title: buxus excisa subsp. excisa
intro: Buxus excisa subsp. excisa is a subspecies of the Buxus excisa plant, also known as the boxwood, a type of evergreen shrub native to South Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Buxus excisa subsp. excisa are thought to include reducing fever, alleviating rheumatic pain, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Buxus excisa subsp. excisa is typically prepared in herbal teas or infusions, as well as being used in traditional folk medicine.
